request.text:
Cristiano Ronaldo dos Santos Aveiro GOIH ComM OIC (Portuguese: Cristiano Ronaldo dos Santos Aveiro; born February 5, 1985), commonly known as Cristiano Ronaldo or simply CR7, is a Portuguese professional footballer who currently serves as captain of both the Saudi Pro League club Al Nassr and the Portugal national football team. He plays as a winger and forward. To date, he has won five Ballon d’Or awards, five FIFA Best Men’s Player titles, and four European Golden Shoe awards. In terms of team honors, he has won seven league titles, five UEFA Champions League trophies, and two major international tournament championships. Ronaldo began his career at Portuguese club Sporting CP and joined the English Premier League side Manchester United at the age of 18. During the 2007–08 season, he scored 31 league goals, surpassing the record previously held by former Manchester United midfielder George Best.

Ronaldo transferred to Real Madrid in 2009. Over nine seasons with the club, he made 438 appearances, scoring 451 goals and providing 132 assists.

In the 2013–14 season, Ronaldo led Real Madrid to victory in the UEFA Champions League and the Copa del Rey, also winning the European Golden Shoe, the UEFA Champions League top scorer award, and the La Liga top scorer award that season. On July 10, 2018, after failing to secure a desired salary increase, he left Real Madrid and joined Italian Serie A club Juventus for a transfer fee of €100 million, winning the Serie A Footballer of the Year award in his first season. In 2021, Ronaldo returned to Manchester United. On December 25, 2022, he reached a transfer agreement with Al Nassr.

In 2011 and 2012, Ronaldo finished second in the FIFA Ballon d’Or voting. He won the Ballon d’Or in 2013, 2014, 2016, and 2017, and was named UEFA Best Player in Europe in 2014, 2016, and 2017.

On January 21, 2021, Ronaldo became the highest goal scorer in football history, with 760 official career goals, and the tally has continued to grow since. On May 19 of the same year, he became the first player to achieve a domestic treble (winning the league title, domestic cup, and domestic super cup) in at least three of Europe’s top five leagues (specifically the Premier League, La Liga, and Serie A). On September 1, he surpassed Iranian striker Ali Daei’s record of 109 international goals by scoring his 111th goal for Portugal, setting a Guinness World Record for the most international goals scored by a male footballer. On December 2, with 801 career goals, Ronaldo became the first footballer in history to surpass 800 official career goals.

According to Forbes’ 2022 list of the world’s highest-paid athletes, Ronaldo ranked first with annual earnings of $450 million. On January 3, 2021, he reached 490 million followers on Instagram, becoming the first individual to surpass 500 million followers on the platform.

On March 23, 2023, Ronaldo became the player with the most appearances in men’s international football history, receiving official recognition from Guinness World Records.

On June 18, 2024, Ronaldo played in Portugal’s UEFA Euro 2024 Group F match against the Czech Republic, becoming the only player to appear in six different European Championships.

On September 5, 2024, Ronaldo scored in a UEFA Nations League match against Croatia, taking his total career goal tally past 900.
